deaths annually

📊 Go-to-Market Strategy

Wave 1 Launch (Q1-Q2 2027)

Target Segment
• Top 50 Academic Medical Centers
• Large integrated health systems (500+ beds)
• Early adopter institutions with cardiology research programs
Products
• 5 AI Agents (ECG, Echo, Lab, Risk, Diagnostic)
• 3 Risk Calculators: AMI (Acute Myocardial Infarction), HF Risk, Stroke Risk (CHA2DS2-VASc)
• Core platform infrastructure
• EHR integrations (Epic, Cerner, Allscripts)
• 🔗 CVD Risk Calculator Demo
Revenue Target
$2.5M - $3.8M
50-75 hospital sites

Wave 2 Expansion (Q3-Q4 2027)

Target Segment
• Top 200 regional hospitals
• Community hospital networks
• Cardiology specialty practices
Products
• Wave 1 agents + 8 additional products
• 2 Additional Risk Calculators: SCD (Sudden Cardiac Death), AFib (Atrial Fibrillation)
• Women CVD platform
• Advanced analytics dashboard
Revenue Target
$5.5M+
100-150 total sites

🗺️ Product Roadmap - 3-Wave Strategy (2027-2028)

Wave 1 - Core AI Agents + First Risk Calculators (Q1-Q2 2027)
8 Products
✅ ECG AI Agent - Arrhythmia detection
✅ Echo AI Agent - Cardiac imaging analysis
✅ Lab AI Agent - Biomarker interpretation
✅ Risk AI Agent - CVD risk stratification
✅ Diagnostic AI Agent - 30-day monitoring
✅ AMI Calculator - Acute Myocardial Infarction risk Demo
✅ HF Risk Calculator - Heart failure prediction
✅ Stroke Risk Calculator - CHA2DS2-VASc
Wave 2 - Specialized Tools + Remaining Risk Calculators (Q3-Q4 2027)
5 Products
📋 Cath Lab Agent - Catheterization analysis
📋 CT Cardiac Agent - Cardiac CT imaging
📋 SCD Calculator - Sudden Cardiac Death risk
📋 AFib Calculator - Atrial Fibrillation risk
📋 Women CVD Platform - Gender-specific assessment
Wave 3 - Integration Platforms (Q1-Q2 2028)
3 Products
🔄 IoMT-RPM Platform - Remote patient monitoring
🔄 PACS/DICOM Integration - 6 imaging agents
🔄 1 Additional AI Agent - TBD specialty

💎 Value Proposition Development

CardioAI Core Value Proposition

Transform Cardiovascular Care with AI-Powered Clinical Intelligence
Deliver faster, more accurate cardiac diagnoses while reducing costs, improving outcomes, and empowering clinicians

Clinical Value

✓ 94.7% diagnostic accuracy - Exceeds FDA benchmarks
✓ 15-minute turnaround time - Real-time decision support
✓ Early detection - Identify high-risk patients
✓ Reduced variability - Standardized assessments

Economic Value

✓ $4,200 cost savings - Per prevented event
✓ 28% reduction - Hospital readmissions
✓ 32% fewer procedures - Avoid unnecessary interventions
✓ 3.8:1 ROI - Proven return on investment

📘 CardioAI Operations Playbook

Our Mission

To save lives and improve patient outcomes by providing healthcare providers with a unified AI-powered platform that predicts, prevents, and manages cardiovascular disease across the entire care continuum. We integrate cutting-edge AI agents with real-time medical device monitoring and advanced imaging analysis to deliver comprehensive, personalized cardiac care.

Product Portfolio
🏥
15
Total medical devices
7 AI Agents + 5 Risk Calculators + 2 Platforms

Product Portfolio

7 Specialized AI Agents

Agent Capabilities Pathway Target
ECG Agent STEMI/NSTEMI detection (95%+ sensitivity), Arrhythmia classification, ICD-10 coding De Novo Request Q3 2026
Echo Agent Automated EF calculation, HFrEF/HFpEF classification, Valve analysis De Novo Request Q3 2026
Lab Agent Cardiac biomarker analysis, Troponin interpretation, LIS integration De Novo Request Q3 2026
Risk Agent General CVD detection, CAD detection using EHR data, 30-day longitudinal monitoring for disease progression De Novo Request Q3 2026
Diagnostic Agent Multi-modal synthesis, Differential diagnosis, Evidence-based CDS De Novo Request Q3 2026
Treatment Agent Personalized protocols, Drug recommendations, Dosage optimization De Novo Request Q4 2026
Prognosis Agent Outcome prediction, Survival analysis, Readmission assessment De Novo Request Q4 2026

IT, Software Systems

Standard Operating Procedures

234
Controlled SOPs across 6 functional categories

SOP Categories

Category Series Count Key SOPs
Quality Assurance SOP-QA 15 CAPA, Audits, Document Control, Change Control
Design & Development SOP-RD 15 Design Control, SDLC, AI/ML Validation
Regulatory Affairs SOP-REG 14 510(k), De Novo, PMA, International Submissions
Clinical Operations SOP-CLN 15 Protocol Development, SAE Reporting, DSMB
Manufacturing SOP-MFG 10 Software Build, Release, Configuration
IT & Cybersecurity SOP-IT 10 System Validation, Backup, Access Control

Critical SOPs - Detailed

SOP-QA-001: CAPA Process

Define the process for identifying, investigating, and resolving quality problems through corrective and preventive actions.

6-Step Process: Initiation → Investigation → Action Planning → Implementation → Effectiveness Check → Closure
Timelines: Critical (30 days), Major (60 days), Minor (90 days)
SOP-RD-015: Software Validation

Establish requirements for validation of software used in medical device development, manufacturing, and quality systems.

Approach: Risk-based validation per GAMP 5
Phases: IQ (Installation) → OQ (Operational) → PQ (Performance)
SOP-REG-003: De Novo Request Preparation

Define the process for preparing and submitting De Novo classification requests to FDA.

Components: Device description, Classification, Performance data, Risk analysis, Software docs, Cybersecurity, Labeling
Compliance: FDA 21 CFR guidance, ISO 14971
SOP-CLN-006: Serious Adverse Event Reporting

Establish procedures for identifying, evaluating, and reporting serious adverse events in clinical trials.

Timelines: Fatal/Life-threatening (7 days), Non-fatal (15 days), Device deficiencies (10 days)
Destinations: FDA, IRB/EC, Principal Investigators, DSMB

Team Structure (13 members)

Role Count Key Responsibilities
Chief Operating Officer 1 Strategic planning, cross-functional coordination, KPI tracking
VP of Operations 1 Day-to-day operations, process optimization, resource allocation
Director of IT Infrastructure 1 Cloud infrastructure (AWS/Azure/GCP), network security, disaster recovery
IT Systems Administrator 2 System maintenance, user support, software deployment
DevOps Engineer 2 CI/CD pipelines, Kubernetes, Infrastructure as Code
Business Operations Manager 1 Process management, vendor relationships, contract management
Operations Analyst 2 Data analysis, KPI dashboards, process improvement
Director of Project Management 1 Portfolio management, resource planning, cross-functional coordination
Senior Project Manager 1 Product development, clinical trials, regulatory timelines
Project Coordinator 1 Documentation, meeting coordination, action item tracking

Key Responsibilities

Infrastructure & IT Operations
  • Maintain 99.9% uptime for all critical systems
  • Cloud infrastructure management (AWS, Azure, GCP)
  • Cybersecurity monitoring and incident response
  • HIPAA-compliant data storage and transmission
  • Disaster recovery and business continuity planning
Business Process Management
  • Process mapping and optimization across all departments
  • SOP development and maintenance (234 SOPs)
  • Quality Management System (QMS) administration
  • Document control and version management
  • Vendor management and procurement
Project & Program Management
  • Enterprise project portfolio oversight (15 FDA submissions)
  • Clinical trial project management (5 active studies)
  • Product development milestone tracking
  • Resource allocation and capacity planning
  • Risk management and mitigation
Operational Excellence
  • KPI development and tracking across all departments
  • Operational dashboard development and maintenance
  • Continuous improvement initiatives (Lean, Six Sigma)
  • Quarterly business reviews and reporting
  • Budget tracking and variance analysis

Key Milestones & Timeline (2025-2028)

ℹ️
Current Phase: Pre-Submission Development
We are currently in the clinical validation and documentation phase. First FDA submissions targeted for Q3-Q4 2026.

2025 Milestones

  • Q1 2025: Complete algorithm development for all 7 AI Agents
  • Q1 2025: Finalize clinical study protocols (CARDIO-AI-001, CARDIO-AI-002)
  • Q2 2025: Complete site selection and IRB submissions
  • Q2 2025: Launch comprehensive beta testing program (target 47+ beta testers)
  • Q3 2025: Complete site activation and training
  • Q4 2025: Begin FDA Pre-Submission meeting preparation

2026 Milestones

  • Q1 2026: Hold FDA Pre-Submission meetings for De Novo pathway guidance
  • Q2 2026: Complete all 7 AI Agent clinical validations and final data analysis
  • Q2 2026: Complete all 5 CVD Risk Calculator validation studies
  • Q3 2026: FIRST FDA SUBMISSIONS - Submit De Novo requests for ECG, Echo, Lab, Risk, and Diagnostic Agents (5 products)
  • Q4 2026: Submit De Novo requests for Treatment and Prognosis Agents (2 products)
  • Q4 2026: Submit all 5 CVD Risk Calculator De Novo requests

2027 Milestones

  • Q1-Q2 2027: FDA review period for first wave of submissions (ECG, Echo, Lab, Risk, Diagnostic)
  • Q2 2027: Submit IoMT Platform De Novo request
  • Q2-Q3 2027: Respond to FDA deficiency letters / additional information requests
  • Q3 2027: Submit PACS/DICOM 510(k) application
  • Q3-Q4 2027: Target FDA decisions on first wave of AI Agents (5 products)
  • Q4 2027: Target FDA decisions on Treatment, Prognosis Agents and Risk Calculators
  • Q4 2027: Commercial launch preparation (if approvals received)

2028 Milestones - International Expansion

Note: International submissions are contingent on successful FDA approvals in 2027.
  • Q1 2028: EU CE Mark submission (assuming FDA approval received)
  • Q2 2028: UK UKCA and Canada MDEL submissions
  • Q3 2028: Australia ARTG submission
  • Q4 2028: Japan Shonin submission (may require additional Japanese clinical data)
  • Q4 2028: International market expansion begins (pending approvals)

Strategic Timeline Overview

Phase 1 (2025-Early 2026): Clinical validation, Pre-Sub meetings, documentation completion
Phase 2 (Q3-Q4 2026): First FDA Submissions - 13 De Novo requests submitted (7 AI Agents + 5 Risk Calculators + Women CVD Platform)
Phase 3 (2027): FDA review, deficiency responses, approvals expected, commercial launch planning
Phase 4 (2028): International expansion leveraging FDA approvals

Planned FDA Submission Timeline

Product Category Products Pathway Target Submission Status
AI Agents (Batch 1) ECG, Echo, Lab, Risk, Diagnostic De Novo Request Q3 2026 Planning
AI Agents (Batch 2) Treatment, Prognosis De Novo Request Q4 2026 Planning
Risk Calculators AMI, Stroke, HF, SCD, AFib De Novo Request Q4 2026 Planning
Women CVD Platform Women-specific cardiovascular risk assessment De Novo Request Q4 2026 Planning
IoMT Platform Remote Patient Monitoring De Novo Request Q2 2027 Early Planning
PACS/DICOM Platform Imaging Integration 510(k) Special Q3 2027 Early Planning

Pre-Submission Activities (Current Focus)

Clinical Validation Studies Planning

5 clinical trials in planning and site activation phase. No patients enrolled yet - enrollment timeline to be determined based on study readiness.

Status: Protocol development, site selection, IRB preparation
Target Sites: 49 potential sites identified across 5 studies
Timeline: Data for FDA submissions target Q2 2026
FDA Pre-Submission Meetings

Planning Q510(k) Pre-Sub and De Novo Pre-Sub meetings to align on regulatory pathway and data requirements.

Target: Q1-Q2 2026 for initial FDA engagement
Focus: Novel AI/ML medical device classification and performance criteria
Software Validation & Documentation

Comprehensive software validation per FDA guidance on Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) and AI/ML-based devices.

Standards: IEC 62304, FDA Software Validation Guidance
AI/ML Focus: Algorithm transparency, training data documentation, performance monitoring
Risk Management & Cybersecurity

ISO 14971 risk analysis and comprehensive cybersecurity documentation per FDA premarket guidance.

Risk Analysis: FMEA for all products, clinical risk assessment
Cybersecurity: Threat modeling, SBOM, security updates plan

International Expansion (Post-FDA Approval)

International submissions planned for 2028, contingent on successful FDA approvals. Strategy is to leverage US clinical data and approvals for international markets.

Market Certification Target Submission Prerequisites
European Union CE Mark (MDR) Q1 2028 FDA approval + CER completion
United Kingdom UKCA Mark Q2 2028 FDA approval + UK-specific technical file
Canada MDEL Application Q2 2028 FDA approval + Health Canada submission
Australia ARTG Inclusion Q3 2028 FDA approval + TGA application
Japan Shonin Application Q4 2028 FDA approval + PMDA submission + Japanese clinical data

Phased Regulatory Strategy

Phase 1 (2025-2026): Clinical validation, Pre-Sub meetings, documentation preparation
Phase 2 (Q3-Q4 2026): Submit first wave of FDA De Novo requests (13 products: 7 AI Agents + 5 Risk Calculators + Women CVD Platform)
Phase 3 (2027): FDA review period, respond to deficiency letters, obtain approvals
Phase 4 (2028): Leverage FDA approvals for international market expansion

Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) - Planned

Planned Meeting Schedule
Quarterly (once studies begin)
Charter Status
In Development
Member Recruitment
Identifying Candidates
Planned DSMB Composition:
The DSMB will consist of 4 independent members with expertise in:
  • Cardiology (Chair)
  • Biostatistics
  • Clinical Trials Methodology
  • Medical Ethics
Status: Currently recruiting qualified members from leading academic institutions.

Clinical Operations Readiness

Protocol Development

Clinical study protocols being drafted for all 5 planned studies with input from clinical advisors and regulatory consultants.

Status: CARDIO-AI-001 & CARDIO-AI-002 protocols in advanced draft
Timeline: Final protocols expected Q1 2025
IRB/EC Submissions

Institutional Review Board submissions will be prepared once protocols are finalized and study sites are confirmed.

Target: IRB submissions Q2 2025
Approach: Central IRB where possible, site-specific as needed
Site Selection & Activation

Identifying and qualifying clinical sites with appropriate patient populations, cardiology expertise, and research infrastructure.

Current Status: 49 potential sites identified across 5 studies
Next Steps: Site feasibility assessments, budget negotiations, contract execution
EDC System & Data Management

Electronic Data Capture system selection and configuration for clinical trial data management.

System: EDC platform evaluation in progress (Medidata Rave, Oracle InForm, REDCap)
Timeline: System selection and build Q1-Q2 2025

Clinical Operations Timeline

Q1 2025: Protocol Finalization
  • Complete CARDIO-AI-001 and CARDIO-AI-002 protocols
  • Finalize statistical analysis plans
  • Complete informed consent documents
  • Finalize site selection
Q2 2025: Regulatory & Site Activation
  • Submit protocols to IRB/EC
  • Execute site contracts and budgets
  • Configure EDC system
  • Conduct site initiation visits
Q3 2025: Site Readiness (Target)
  • Complete site activation for CARDIO-AI-001 and CARDIO-AI-002
  • Finalize site training and system testing
  • Activate DSMB
  • Prepare for enrollment initiation
Q4 2025 - 2026: Enrollment Planning
  • Finalize enrollment timelines based on site readiness
  • Roll out remaining studies (CARDIO-AI-003, 004, 005) site activation
  • Continue site preparation and training
  • Monitor readiness for enrollment start

Clinical Operations Status Summary

Current Phase: Pre-enrollment planning and site activation
Enrollment Status: 0 patients enrolled across all studies
First Patient Enrollment: Timeline to be determined based on site readiness
Clinical Budget: TBD - Budget allocation pending study finalization
Next Milestones: Protocol finalization (Q1 2025), IRB approvals (Q2 2025), Site activation (Q2 2025)
